target.title: fix(agents): block raw self-update package installs
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: agent self-update requests could run raw global package-manager upgrades such as `sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest`, replacing package files while the old Gateway process kept running.
- Why it matters: stale running gateways can later lazy-import removed hashed runtime chunks, producing `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` or silent channel hangs.
- What changed: the exec tool now blocks raw global package-manager installs/updates of the `openclaw` package and points operators toward `update.run` / `openclaw update`, which owns restart handling.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): normal `openclaw update` is still allowed, local non-global `npm install openclaw` is not blocked, and non-OpenClaw packages are unaffected.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #64892
- Related #24085, #60439
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: raw `npm install -g openclaw@latest` style self-updates are rejected by `exec` before process launch.
- Real environment tested: local Ubuntu workspace, Node 24.15.0, pnpm 10.33.2.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): targeted regression test passed with 59 passed / 4 skipped.
- Observed result after fix: direct, sudo-wrapped, shell-wrapped, and `env -S` raw OpenClaw package upgrades are rejected; `openclaw update`, local `npm install openclaw`, and `@openclaw/sdk` global installs remain unblocked by this guard.
- What was not tested: a live service-managed Gateway self-update flow.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #64892 includes journal evidence of `sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest` followed by stale chunk import failures.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the system prompt told agents to use `update.run`, but the exec surface did not enforce that guidance and still allowed raw global OpenClaw package-manager upgrades.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no exec-side parser blocked global `npm`/`pnpm`/`bun`/`yarn` installs or updates of the `openclaw` package.
- Contributing context (if known): package-manager upgrades can replace `dist/` while a service-managed Gateway keeps the old process image alive.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: exec rejects raw global OpenClaw package-manager upgrades, including common wrappers, without blocking `openclaw update` or unrelated package installs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is command-selection enforcement at the exec boundary, so a focused parser/tool test covers the behavior before any host command runs.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing channel-login guard tests covered the same exec-control-command pattern but not self-update package-manager installs.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Agents can no longer use `exec` to run raw global package-manager upgrades of the `openclaw` package. They must use `update.run` or `openclaw update` for self-updates.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[user asks self-update] -> exec: npm install -g openclaw@latest -> package files replaced -> old gateway imports missing chunks

After:
[user asks self-update] -> exec rejects raw package-manager upgrade -> agent uses update.run/openclaw update -> supported restart path
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this narrows command execution by blocking one unsafe self-update class and directing users to the supported update path.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu
- Runtime/container: Node 24.15.0, pnpm 10.33.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): exec tool / agent self-update
- Relevant config (redacted): default local test config

### Steps

1. Create an exec tool with gateway host execution.
2. Attempt `sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest`.
3. Attempt wrapped variants such as `sudo -u openclaw bash -lc 'pnpm add --global openclaw@latest'` and `env -S 'npm install -g openclaw@latest'`.

### Expected

- The exec tool rejects the raw package-manager OpenClaw upgrade before launch and tells the agent to use `update.run` / `openclaw update`.

### Actual

- Matches expected in the added regression tests.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: raw npm/pnpm/bun/yarn global OpenClaw package upgrades are detected; sudo/shell/env wrappers are rejected; allowed non-target commands stay allowed by the parser.
- Edge cases checked: `openclaw update`, local `npm install openclaw`, and `npm install -g @openclaw/sdk` are not classified as raw OpenClaw self-upgrades.
- What you did **not** verify: live service-managed Gateway update/restart behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a legitimate operator expected the agent to run raw `npm install -g openclaw` through exec.
  - Mitigation: the error message points to `update.run` / `openclaw update`, the supported path that restarts the gateway safely.
target.author: wt-wsl
